name: compare-code
description: Two fenced code blocks side-by-side, each with a label.
purpose: Use to contrast a before/after refactor, two API styles, or two configurations. Each side gets an h3 label and one fenced block.
skeleton:
<!-- _class: compare-code -->

## Heading framing the comparison.

### Before

```js
function before() {
  return 'old';
}
```

### After

```js
function after() {
  return 'new';
}
```
sample:
<!-- _class: compare-code -->

`Before & after · Component manifest loading`

## Flat-file lookup versus folder-shape lookup.

`Before · flat file`

```js
const fs = require('node:fs');
const path = require('node:path');

function loadOne(name) {
  const p = path.join(
    __dirname, 'lib', 'components',
    `${name}.json`
  );
  return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8'));
}

const cards = loadOne('cards-grid');
```

`After · folder shape`

```js
const fs = require('node:fs');
const path = require('node:path');

function loadOne(name) {
  const p = path.join(
    __dirname, 'lib', 'components',
    name, 'manifest.json'
  );
  return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8'));
}

const cards = loadOne('cards-grid');
```

whenToUse.title: Concrete code on both sides
whenToUse.body: Both sides hold short, readable snippets — refactor before/after, two API styles, two configurations. The diff is the point of the slide.

whenToUse.title: Equal-length snippets
whenToUse.body: Snippets render side-by-side. Wildly different lengths break the visual balance — trim aggressively or split into two slides.

whenToUse.title: Names the change
whenToUse.body: The h3 label on each side names what the reader is looking at (Before, After, v1, v2). Without labels the audience has to infer.

antiPatterns.title: One side is prose
antiPatterns.body: If one column is code and the other is description, use a single fenced block with surrounding prose. compare-code is for code-versus-code.

antiPatterns.title: Snippets longer than 14 lines
antiPatterns.body: The text shrinks below readability past 14 lines per side. Split into two slides or extract the key delta into a smaller diff.

antiPatterns.title: Three-way comparison
antiPatterns.body: compare-code is binary. For three configurations or three implementations, use prose with successive fenced blocks or a `compare-table`.

related.name: before-after
related.when: the change is state, not code

related.name: redline
related.when: the change is in verbatim text or legal language

related.name: compare-table
related.when: three or more variants on shared dimensions

mirror.label: Mirror — swap left and right
mirror.caption: Flips the left and right columns. Useful when the deck's visual rhythm wants the after-state on the left, or when the natural reading order is new-then-old.
mirror.sample:
<!-- _class: compare-code mirror -->

`After & before · Component manifest loading`

## Folder-shape lookup, with the prior approach for reference.

`After · folder shape`

```js
function loadOne(name) {
  const p = path.join(
    __dirname, 'lib', 'components',
    name, 'manifest.json'
  );
  return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8'));
}
```

`Before · flat file`

```js
function loadOne(name) {
  const p = path.join(
    __dirname, 'lib', 'components',
    `${name}.json`
  );
  return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8'));
}
```
